name: Credit.Org - Riverside
description_short: National nonprofit credit counseling and HUD-approved housing counselor network offering free debt relief, credit building, and homeownership guidance since 1974.
description_long:
Credit.org is a established nonprofit organization that has operated since 1974, providing free financial counseling and housing assistance services across the United States. The organization claims to have delivered $2.2 billion in homeowner support and completed over 5.2 million counseling sessions, positioning itself as a major player in nonprofit financial counseling.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of services including debt relief counseling, credit repair guidance, housing counseling (pre-purchase, foreclosure prevention, reverse mortgage), rental readiness assistance, and bankruptcy education. They provide both one-on-one counseling sessions and group educational resources through their FIT Academy platform, which includes structured courses, live webinars, and financial calculators covering budgeting, debt management, mortgage readiness, and homeownership preparation.

Credit.org distinguishes itself through HUD certification and what appears to be a national network structure with partnerships across community organizations. They offer reverse mortgage counseling as a specialized service (both HECM and non-HECM), disaster recovery financial assistance, and workplace financial wellness programs. The organization operates bilingually (English/Spanish) and maintains a professional testimonial-driven marketing approach.

As a free-help nonprofit, Credit.org's primary limitation is that it operates as a counseling and education provider rather than a direct service provider—clients must implement recommendations themselves or work with other organizations. The website emphasizes free consultations but does not disclose specific outcomes, success rates, or detailed information about how their debt relief plans compare to alternatives. Consumers should verify HUD/NFCC certification status independently and understand that nonprofit counseling, while free, requires active client participation.
pros: Completely free debt relief and housing counseling with no hidden fees or service charges
HUD-approved housing counselor services for reverse mortgages, foreclosure prevention, and pre-purchase education
Established 50+ year history (since 1974) with claimed track record of 5.2+ million counseling sessions completed
Bilingual services (English/Spanish) and multilingual website support
Comprehensive free educational platform (FIT Academy) with on-demand courses, live webinars, and financial calculators
Specialized reverse mortgage counseling including both HECM and non-HECM options
Covers diverse financial needs including debt management, bankruptcy education, rental assistance, and disaster recovery
Workplace financial wellness program offerings for employers
cons: Website does not clearly specify NFCC certification status or individual counselor credentials, requiring independent verification
No published success metrics, debt payoff timelines, or outcome data compared to other nonprofit counselors
Debt relief services appear to be advisory/educational only—organization does not directly negotiate with creditors or manage payment plans
Limited transparency on how personalized plans differ from standard nonprofit counseling approaches
No pricing or cost structure information provided despite marketing emphasis on 'free' services (unclear if all services are free or if some have charges)
best_for: Homeowners seeking HUD-approved reverse mortgage counseling and education before committing to a loan
First-time homebuyers wanting free pre-purchase education and down payment guidance
Renters struggling with affordability or needing help qualifying for housing in competitive markets
Individuals in or at-risk of foreclosure seeking free prevention counseling and options guidance
diagnosis: Credit.org is best for individuals seeking free, nonprofit credit and housing counseling without pressure to purchase additional products. The main caveat is that this organization provides education and guidance only—it does not directly manage debt payments, negotiate with creditors, or provide the hands-on implementation that for-profit debt relief companies offer. Consumers must be self-motivated to follow through on personalized plans independently or with other service providers.
services: Free one-on-one debt relief counseling sessions
HUD-approved reverse mortgage counseling (HECM and non-HECM)
Pre-purchase homeownership counseling and down payment education
Credit building and credit report review guidance
Debt management plan creation and counseling
Rental readiness preparation and rental assistance counseling
FIT Academy structured self-paced courses on budgeting, debt, credit, and homeownership
Financial calculators for budgeting, debt payoff, and homeownership planning
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
